verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the former discourse, so far as the subject impressed us, and so far as our poverty of understanding attained to, we have spoken by occasion of the words of the Gospel, where it is written: “The Son cannot do anything of Himself, but what He seeth the Father doing,”—what it is for the Son—that is, the Word, for the Son is the Word—“to see;” and as all things were made by the Word, how it is to be understood that the Son first sees the Father doing, and then only Himself also doeth the things which He has seen done, seeing that the Father has done nothing except by the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have not, however, delivered to you anything as fully explained, and that because we have not understood anything thus clearly set forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, indeed, speech sometimes fails even where the understanding makes way; how much more doth speech suffer defect, where the understanding has nothing perfect!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, therefore, as the Lord gives us, let us briefly run over the passage, and even to-day complete the due task.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Should there perchance remain somewhat of time or of strength, we will reconsider (so far as it may be practicable for us and with you) what it is for the Word “to see” and “to be shown to;” since, in fact, all that is here spoken is such that, if understood according to man’s sense, carnally, the soul full of vain fancies makes for us only certain images of the Father and the Son, just as of two men, the one showing, the other seeing; the one speaking, the other hearing,—all which are idols of the heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if now at length idols have been cast down from their own temples, how much more ought they to be cast down from Christian hearts!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Son,” saith He, “cannot do anything of Himself, but what He sees the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is true: hold this fast, while at the same time ye do not let slip what ye have gotten in the beginning of the Gospel, that “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God,” and especially that “all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Join this that ye have now heard to that hearing, and let both agree together in your hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, except what He seeth the Father doing,” is yet in such wise that what the Father doeth, He doeth only by the Son, because the Son is His Word: and, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” also, “All things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what things soever He doeth, the Son also doeth in like manner; not other things, but these and not in a different, but in like manner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the Father loveth the Son, and showeth Him all things that Himself doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To that which He said above, “except what He seeth the Father doing,” seems to belong this also, “He showeth Him all things that Himself doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if the Father doth show what He doeth, and the Son cannot do except the Father hath shown, and if the Father cannot show unless He hath done, it will follow that it is not through the Son that the Father doeth all things; moreover, if we hold it fixed and unshaken, that the Father doeth all by the Son, then He shows the Son before He doeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the Father doth show to the Son after He has done, that the Son may do the things shown, which being shown were already done, then doubtless something there is that the Father doeth without the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Father doeth not anything without the Son, because the Son of God is God’s Word, and all things were made by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It remains, then, that possibly what the Father is about to do, He shows as about to be done, that it may be done by the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the Son doeth those things which the Father showeth as already done, surely it is not by the Son that the Father hath done the things which He thus showeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they could not be shown to the Son unless they were first done, and the Son would not be able to do them unless they were first shown; therefore were they made without the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet it is a true thing, “All things were made by Him;” therefore they were shown before they were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this we said must be put off, and returned to after briefly scanning the passage, if, as we said, some portion of time and of strength should remain to us for a reconsideration of the matters deferred.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Attend now to a wider and more difficult question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And greater works than these,” saith He, “will He show Him, that ye may marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Greater than these.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Greater than which?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The answer readily occurs: than the cures of bodily diseases which ye have just heard: For the whole occasion of this discourse arose about the man who was thirty and eight years in infirmity, and was healed by the word of Christ; and in respect of this cure, the Lord could say, “Greater works than these He will show Him, that ye may marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there are greater, and the Father will show them to the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not “hath shown,” as of a thing past, but “will show,” of a thing future; or, is about to show.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again a difficult question arises: Why, then, is there something with the Father that has not yet been shown to the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is there something with the Father that was still hid from the Son when He spoke these words?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For surely, if it be “will show,” that is to say, “is about to show,” then He has not yet shown; and He is about to show to the Son at the same time as to these persons, since it follows, “that ye may marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this is a thing hard to see, how the Eternal Father doth show something, as it were in time, to the coeternal Son, who knoweth all things that are with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what are the greater works?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For perhaps this is easy to understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For as the Father,” saith He, “raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, even so the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To raise the dead, then, are greater works than to heal the sick.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But “as the Father raiseth the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, the Father some, the Son others?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But all things are by Him: therefore the Son the same persons as the Father doth; since the Son doeth not other things and in a different manner, but “these” and in “like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus clearly it must be understood, and thus held.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But keep in memory that “the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, too, know not only the power of the Son, but also the will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both the Son quickeneth whom He will, and also the Father quickeneth whom He will—the Son the same persons as the Father; and hence the power of the Father and of the Son is the same, and also the will is the same.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What follows then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son, that all men may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father:” this He subjoined, as rendering a reason of the foregoing sentence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great question comes before us; give it your earnest attention.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son quickeneth whom He will, the Father quickeneth whom He will; the Son raiseth the dead, just as the Father raiseth the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And further, “the Father judgeth not any man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the dead must be raised in the judgment, how can it be said that the Father raiseth the dead, if He judgeth not any man, since “He hath given all judgment to the Son”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in that judgment the dead are raised; some rise to life, others to punishment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Son doeth all this, but the Father not, inasmuch as “He judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son,” it will appear contrary to what has been said, viz., “As the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently the Father and the Son raise together; if they raise together, they quicken together: hence they judge together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, is that true, “For the Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Meanwhile let the questions now proposed engage your minds; the Lord will cause that, when solved, they will delight you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so it is, brethren: every question, unless it stirs the mind to reflection, will not give delight when explained.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May the Lord Himself then follow with us, in case He may perhaps reveal Himself somewhat in those matters which He foldeth up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He foldeth up His light with a cloud; and it is difficult to fly like an eagle above every obscure mist with which the whole earth is covered, and to behold the most serene light in the words of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In case, then, He may perhaps dissipate our darkness with the heat of His rays, and deign to reveal Himself somewhat in the sequel, let us, deferring these questions, look at what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whoso honoreth not the Son, honoreth not the Father that sent Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is a truth, and is plain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, then, “all judgment hath He given to the Son,” as He said above, “that all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father,” what if there be those who honor the Father and honor not the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It cannot be, saith He: “Whoso honoreth not the Son, honoreth not the Father that sent Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One cannot therefore say, I honored the Father, because I knew not the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou didst not yet honor the Son, neither didst thou honor the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is honoring the Father, unless it be in that He hath a Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is one thing when thou art taught to honor God in that He is God; but another thing when thou art taught to honor Him in that He is Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou art taught to honor Him in that He is God, it is as the Creator, as the Almighty, as the Spirit supreme, eternal, invisible, unchangeable, that thou art led to think of Him; but when thou art taught to honor Him in that He is Father, it is the same thing as to honor the Son; because Father cannot be said if there be not a Son, as neither can Son if there be not a Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But lest, it may be, thou honorest the Father indeed as greater, but the Son as less,—as thou mayest say to me, “I do honor the Father, for I know that He has a Son; nor do I err in the name Father, for I do not understand Father without Son, and yet the Son also I honor as the less,”—the Son Himself sets thee right, and recalls thee, saying, “that all may honor the Son,” not in a lower degree, but “as they honor the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “whoso honoreth not the Son, honoreth not the Father that sent Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I,” sayest thou, “wish to give greater honor to the Father, less to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therein thou takest away honor from the Father, wherein thou givest less to the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, being thus minded, it must really seem to thee that the Father either would not or could not beget a Son equal to Himself: if He would not, He lacked the will; if He could not, He lacked the ability.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou not therefore see that, being thus minded, wherein thou wouldst give greater honor to the Father, therein thou art reproachful to the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, so honor the Son as thou honorest the Father, if thou wouldest honor both the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whoso heareth my word, and believeth on Him that sent me, hath eternal life, and cometh not into judgment, but is passed,” not is passing now, but is already passed, “from death into life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And mark this, “Whoso heareth my word, and”—He says not, believeth me, but—“believeth Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him hear the word of the Son, that he may believe the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why heareth Thy word, and yet believeth another?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When we hear any one’s word, is it not him that utters the word we believe? is it not to him who speaks we lend our faith?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, did He mean, saying, “Whoso heareth my word, and believeth Him that sent me,” if it be not this, because “His word is in me”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is “heareth my word,” but “heareth me”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, too, “believeth Him that sent me,” because, believing Him, he believeth His word; but again, believing His word, he believeth me, because I am the Word of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is therefore peace in the Scriptures, and all things duly disposed, and in no way clashing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cast away, then, contention from thy heart; understand the harmony of the Scriptures.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou think that the Truth should speak things contrary to itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whoso heareth my word, and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life, and cometh not into judgment, but is passed from death unto life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You remember what we laid down above, that “as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is beginning already to reveal Himself; and behold, even now, the dead are rising.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “whoso heareth my word, and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life, and will not come into judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Prove that he has risen again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But is passed,” saith He “from death unto life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that is passed from death unto life, has surely without any doubt risen again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he could not pass from death to life, unless he were first in death and not in life; but when he will have passed, he will be in life, and not in death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was therefore dead, and is alive again; he was lost, but is found.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence a resurrection does take place now, and men pass from a death to a life; from the death of infidelity to the life of faith; from the death of falsehood to the life of truth; from the death of iniquity to the life of righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is, therefore, that which is a resurrection of the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May He open the same more fully, and dawn upon us as He begins to do!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We did look for a resurrection of the dead in the end, for so we have believed; yea, not we looked, but are manifestly bound to look for it: for it is not a false thing we believe, when we believe that the dead will rise in the end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord Jesus, then, was willing to make known to us a resurrection of the dead before the resurrection of the dead, it is not as that of Lazarus, or of the widow’s son, or of the ruler of the synagogue’s daughter, who were raised to die again (for in their case there was a resurrection of the dead before the resurrection of the dead); but, as He says here, “hath,” says He, “eternal life, and cometh not into judgment, but is passed from death into life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To life eternal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not, then, as the body of Lazarus: for he indeed passed from the death of the tomb to the life of men, but not to life eternal, seeing he was to die again; whereas the dead, that are to rise again at the end of the world, will pass to eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When our Lord Jesus Christ, then, our heavenly Master, the Word of the Father, and the Truth, was willing to represent to us a resurrection of the dead to eternal life before the resurrection of the dead to eternal life, “The hour cometh,” saith He.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Doubtless thou, imbued with a faith of the resurrection of the flesh, didst look for the hour of the end of the world, which, that thou shouldst not look for here, He added, “and now is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He saith not this, “The hour cometh,” of that last hour, when “at the command and the voice of the archangel and the trump of God, the Lord Himself shall descend from heaven, and the dead in Christ shall rise first; then we who are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet Christ in the air: and so shall we be ever with the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That hour will come, but is not now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But consider what this hour is: “The hour cometh, and now is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What happens in that hour?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, but a resurrection of the dead?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what kind of resurrection?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such that they who rise live for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This will be also in the last hour.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do we understand these two resurrections?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do we, it may be, understand that they who rise now will not rise then; that the resurrection of some is now, of some others then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we have risen in this resurrection, if we have rightly believed; and we ourselves, who have already risen, are looking for another resurrection in the end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, both now are we risen to eternal life, if we perseveringly continue in the same faith; and then, too, we shall rise to eternal life, when we shall be made equal with the angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let Himself distinguish and open up what we have made bold to speak; how there happens to be a resurrection before a resurrection, not of different but of the same persons; nor like that of Lazarus, but into eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He will open it clearly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear ye the Master, while dawning upon us, and as our Sun gliding in upon our hearts; not such as the eyes of flesh desire to look upon, but on whom the eyes of the heart fervently long to be opened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To Him, then, let us give ear: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour cometh, and now is, when the dead”—you see that a resurrection is asserted—“shall hear the voice of the Son of God; and they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why hath He added, “they that hear shall live”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, could they hear unless they lived?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It would have been enough, then, to say, “The hour cometh, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We should immediately understand them to be living, since they could not hear unless they lived.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No, saith He, not because they live they hear; but by hearing they come to life again: “Shall hear, and they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is “shall hear,” but “shall obey”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, as to the hearing of the ear, not all who hear shall live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many, indeed, hear and do not believe; by hearing and not believing, they obey not; by not obeying, they live not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so here, they that “shall hear” are they that “shall obey.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They that obey, then, shall live: let them be sure and certain of it, shall live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ, the Word of God, is preached to us; the Son of God, by whom all things were made, who, for the dispensation’s sake, surely took flesh, was born of a virgin, was an infant in the flesh, a young man in the flesh, suffering in the flesh, dying in the flesh, rising again in the flesh, ascending in the flesh, promising a resurrection to the flesh, promising a resurrection to the mind—to the mind before the flesh, to the flesh after the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whoso heareth and obeyeth, shall live; whoso heareth and obeyeth not, that is, heareth and despiseth, heareth and believeth not, shall not live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why shall not live?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because he heareth not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “heareth not”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Obeyeth not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, then, “they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Turn your thoughts now to what we said had to be deferred, that it may now, if possible, be opened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Concerning this very resurrection He immediately subjoined, “For as the Father hath life in Himself, even so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means that, “The Father hath life in Himself”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not elsewhere hath He life but in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His living, in fact, is in Him, not from elsewhere, nor derived from another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not, as it were, borrow life, nor, as it were, become a partaker of life, of a life which is not what Himself is: but “hath life in Himself,” so that the very life is to Him His very self.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I should be able yet further in some small measure to speak from this matter, by proposing examples for informing your understanding, will depend on God’s help and the piety of your attention.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God lives, and the soul also lives; but the life of God is unchangeable, the life of the soul is changeable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In God is neither increase nor decrease; but He is the same always in Himself, is ever as He is: not in one way now, in another way hereafter, in some other way before.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the life of the soul is exceedingly various: it lived foolish, it lives wise; it lived unrighteous, it lives righteous; now remembers, now forgets; now learns, now cannot learn; now loses what it had learned, now apprehends what it had lost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The life of the soul is changeable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when the soul lives in unrighteousness, that is its death; when again it becomes righteous, it becomes partaker of another life, which is not what itself is, inasmuch as by rising up to God, and cleaving to God, of Him it is justified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is said, “To him that believeth on Him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted for righteousness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By forsaking God, it becomes unrighteous; by coming to Him, it is made righteous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does it not seem to thee as it were something cold, which, when brought near the fire, grows warm; when removed from the fire, grows cold?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A something dark, which, brought near the light, grows bright; when removed from the light, grows dark?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Something such is the soul: God is not any such thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, man may say that he has light now in his eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let thine eyes say then, if they can, as by a voice of their own, “We have light in ourselves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I answer: Not correctly do you say that you have light in yourselves: you have light, but in the heavens; you have light, but in the moon, in candles, if it happen to be night, not in yourselves: for, being shut, you lose what you perceive when open.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not in yourselves have you light; keep the light if you can when the sun is set: ’tis night, enjoy the light of night; keep the light when the candle is withdrawn; but since you remain in darkness when the candle is withdrawn, you have not light in yourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently, to have light in oneself is not to need light from another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, whoso understands wherein He shows that the Son is equal with the Father, when He saith, “As the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given to the Son also to have life in Himself;” that there may be only this difference between the Father and the Son, that the Father hath life in Himself, which none gave Him, whilst the Son hath life in Himself which the Father gave.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But here also arises a cloud that must be scattered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not lose heart, let us strive in earnest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here are pastures of the mind; let us not disdain them, that we may live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, sayest thou, thyself confessest that the Father hath given life to the Son, that He may have life in Himself, even as the Father hath life in Himself; that the Father not lacking, the Son may not lack; that as the Father is life, so the Son may be life; and both united one life, not two lives; because God is one, not two Gods; and this same is to be life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, is the Father said to have given life to the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not so as if the Son had been without life before, and received life from the Father that He might live; for if it were so, He would not have life in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, I was speaking of the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul exists; though it be not wise, though it be not righteous, though it be not godly, it is soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is one thing for it to be soul, but another thing to be wise, to be righteous, to be godly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Something there is, then, in which it is not yet wise, not yet righteous, not yet godly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless it is not therefore nothing, it is not therefore non-life; for it shows itself to be alive by certain of its own actions, although it does not show itself to be wise, godly, or righteous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if it were not living it would not move the body, would not command the feet to walk, the hands to work, the eyes to look, the ears to hear; would not open the mouth for speaking, nor move the tongue to distinction of speech.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, then, by these operations it shows itself to have life, and to be something which is better than the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But does it in any wise show itself by these operations to be wise, godly, or righteous?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not the foolish, the wicked, the unrighteous walk, work, see, hear, speak?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the soul rises to something which itself is not, which is above itself, and from which its being is, then it gets wisdom, righteousness, holiness, which so long as it was without, it was dead, and did not have the life by which itself should live, but only that by which the body was quickened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that in the soul by which the body is quickened is one thing, that by which the soul itself is quickened is another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Better, certainly, than the body is the soul, but better than the soul itself is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul, even if it be foolish, ungodly, unrighteous, is the life of the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since its own life is God, just as it supplies vigor, comeliness, activity, the functions of the limbs to the body, while it exists in the body; so, in like manner, while God, its life, is in the soul, He supplies to it wisdom, godliness, righteousness, charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, what the soul supplies to the body, and what God supplies to the soul, are of a different kind: the soul quickens and is quickened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It quickens while dead, even if itself is not quickened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the word comes, and is poured into the hearers, and they not only hear, but are made obedient, the soul rises from its death to its life—that is, from unrighteousness, from folly, from ungodliness, to its God, who is to it wisdom, righteousness, light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it rise to Him, and be enlightened by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Come near,” saith he, “to Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what shall we have?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And be enlightened.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, by “coming to” ye are enlightened, and by “departing from” ye become darkened, your light was not in yourselves, but in your God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come to Him that ye may rise again: if ye depart from Him, ye shall die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If by coming to Him ye live, and by departing from Him ye die, your life was not in yourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the same is your life which is your light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because with Thee is the fountain of life, and in Thy light we shall see light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not, then, in like manner as the soul is one thing before it is enlightened, and becomes a better thing when it is enlightened, by participation of a better; not so, I say, was the Word of God, the Son of God, something else before He received life, that He should have life by participation; but He has life in Himself, and is consequently Himself the very life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it, then, that He saith, “hath given to the Son to have life in Himself”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I would say it briefly, He begot the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is not that He existed without life, and received life, but He is life by being begotten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father is life not by being begotten; the Son is life by being begotten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father is of no father; the Son is of God the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father in His being is of none, but in that He is Father, ’tis because of the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Son also, in that He is Son, ’tis because of the Father: in His being, He is of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This He said, therefore: “hath given life to the Son, that He might have it in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as if He were to say, “The Father, who is life in Himself, begot the Son, who should be life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Indeed, He would have this dedit (hath given) to be understood for the same thing as genuit (hath begotten).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is like as if we said to a person, “God hath given thee being.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If to some one already existing, then He gave him not being, because he who could receive existed before it was given him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, thou hearest it said, “He gave thee being,” thou wast not in being to receive, but thou didst receive, that thou shouldst be by coming into existence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The builder gave to this house that it should be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what did he give to it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He gave it to be a house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what did he give?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Gave it what?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To be a house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How could he give to a house that it should be a house?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the house was, to what did he give to be a house, when the house existed already?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, does that mean, “gave it to be a house”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It means, he brought to pass that it should be a house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, then, what gave He to the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Gave Him to be the Son, begot Him to be life—that is, “gave Him to have life in Himself” that He should be the life not needing life, that He may not be understood as having life by participation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He had life by par ticipation, He might, by losing, be without life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not take, nor think, nor believe this to be possible respecting the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the Father continues the life, the Son continues the life: the Father, life in Himself, not from the Son; the Son, life in Himself, but from the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Begotten of the Father, that He might live in Himself; but the Father, not begotten, life in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor did He beget the Son less than Himself to become equal by growth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For surely He by whom, being perfect, the times were created, was not assisted by time towards His own perfection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before all time, He is co-eternal with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father has never been without the Son; but the Father is eternal, therefore also the Son co-eternal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Soul, what of thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wast dead, didst lose life; hear then the Father through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Arise, take to thee life, that in Him who has life in Himself thou mayest receive the life which is not in thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that giveth thee life, then, is the Father and the Son; and the first resurrection is accomplished when thou risest to partake of the life which thou art not thyself, and by partaking art made living.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rise from thy death to thy life, which is thy God, and pass from death to eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father hath eternal life in Himself; and unless He had begotten such a Son as had life in Himself, it could not be that as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son should quicken whom He will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what of that resurrection of the body?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these who hear and live, whence live, except by hearing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “the friend of the Bridegroom standeth and heareth Him, and rejoiceth greatly because of the Bridegroom’s voice:” not because of his own voice; that is to say, they hear and live by partaking, not by coming into being; and all that hear live, because all that obey live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Tell us something, O Lord, also of the resurrection of the flesh; for there have been those who denied it, asserting that this is the only resurrection which is wrought by faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of which resurrection the Lord has just now made mention, and inflamed our desire, because “the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God, and shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not some of those who hear shall live, and others shall die; but “all that hear shall live,” because all that obey shall live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, we see a resurrection of the mind; let us not therefore let go our faith of the resurrection of the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And unless Thou, O Lord Jesus, declare to us this, whom shall we oppose to those who assert the contrary?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For truly all sects that have undertaken to engraft any religion upon men have allowed this resurrection of minds; otherwise, it might be said to them, If the soul rise not, why speakest thou to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What meanest thou to do in me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou dost not make of the worse a better, why speakest thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou dost not make a righteous of the unrighteous, why speakest thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou dost make righteous of the unrighteous, godly of the ungodly, wise of the foolish, thou confessest that my soul doth rise again, if I comply with thee and believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, then, all those that have founded any sect, even of false religion, while they wished to be believed, could not but admit this resurrection of minds: all have agreed concerning this; but many have denied the resurrection of the flesh, and affirmed that the resurrection had taken place already in faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such the apostle resisteth, saying, “Of whom is Hymeneus and Philetus, who concerning the truth have erred, saying that the resurrection hath taken place already, and overthrow the faith of some.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They said that the resurrection had taken place already, but in such manner that another was not to be expected; and they blamed people who were looking for a resurrection of the flesh, just as if the resurrection which was promised were already accomplished in the act of believing, namely, in the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle censures these.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why does he censure them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did they not affirm what the Lord spoke just now: “The hour cometh, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God, and they that hear shall live”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, saith Jesus to thee, it is of the life of minds that I am hitherto speaking: I am not yet speaking of the life of bodies; but I speak of the life of that which is the life of bodies, that is, of the life of souls, in which the life of bodies exists.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I know that there are bodies lying in the tombs; I know also that your bodies will lie in the tombs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am not speaking of that resurrection, but I speak of this; in this, rise ye again, lest ye rise to punishment in that.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that ye may know that I speak also of that, what do I add?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For as the Father hath life in Himself, even so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This life which the Father is, which the Son is, to what does it pertain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the soul or to the body?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not surely the body that is sensible of that life of wisdom, but the rational mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For not every soul hath capacity to apprehend wisdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A brute beast, in fact, has a soul, but the soul of the brute beast cannot apprehend wisdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is the human soul, then, that can perceive this life which the Father hath in Himself, and hath given to the Son to have in Himself; because that is “the true light which enlighteneth,” not every soul, but “every man coming into this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, I speak to the mind itself, let it hear, that is, let it obey and live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, keep not silent, O Lord, concerning the resurrection of the flesh; lest men believe it not, and we continue reasoners, not preachers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But “as the Father hath life in Himself, even so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them that hear, understand; let them believe that they may understand; let them obey that they may live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that they may not suppose that the resurrection is finished here, let them hear this further: “and hath given Him authority to execute judgment also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who hath given?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom hath He given?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the Son; namely, to whom He gave to have life in Himself, to the same hath He given authority to execute judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because He is the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is the Christ, both Son of God and Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This was in the beginning with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, how He hath given Him to have life in Himself!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because “the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us,” was made man of the Virgin Mary, He is the Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, therefore, hath He received as Son of man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Authority to execute judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That in the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then also there will be a resurrection, but a resurrection of bodies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, then, God raiseth up souls by Christ, the Son of God; bodies He raiseth up by the same Christ, the Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Hath given Him authority.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He should not have this authority did He not receive it; and He should be a man without authority.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the same who is Son of God is also Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by adhering to the unity of person, the Son of man with the Son of God is made one person, and the Son of God is the same person which the Son of man is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what characteristic it has, and wherefore, must be distinguished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son of man has soul and body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son of God, which is the Word of God, has man, as the soul has body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And just as soul having body does not make two persons, but one man; so the Word, having man, maketh not two persons, but one Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A rational soul, having a body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word of God, having man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see of what things I speak, who I the speaker am, and to whom I am speaking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now hear concerning the resurrection of bodies, not me, but the Lord about to speak, on account of those who have risen again by a resurrection from death, by cleaving to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To a life which knows not death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why knows not death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because it knows not mutability.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why knows not mutability?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because it is life in itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And hath given Him authority to execute judgment, because He is the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What judgment, what kind of judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Marvel not at this” which I have said,—gave Him authority to execute judgment,—“for the hour is coming.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not adds “and now is:” therefore He means to make known to us a certain hour in the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The hour is now that the dead rise, the hour will be in the end of the world that the dead rise: but that they rise now in the mind, then in the flesh; that they rise now in the mind by the Word of God, the Son of God; then in the flesh by the Word of God made flesh, the Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it will not be the Father Himself that will come to judgment, notwithstanding the Father doth not withdraw Himself from the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, is it that the Father Himself will not come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that He will not be seen in the judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They shall look on Him whom they pierced.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That form which stood before the judge, will be Judge: that form will judge which was judged; for it was judged unjustly, it will judge justly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There will come the form of a servant, and that same will be apparent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how could the form of God be made apparent to the just and to the unjust?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the judgment were to be only among the just, then the form of God might appear as to the just.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because the judgment is to be of the just and of the unjust, and that it is not permitted to the wicked to see God,—for “blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God,”—such a Judge will appear as may be seen by those whom He is about to crown, and by those whom He is about to condemn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence the form of a servant will be seen, the form of God will be hid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son of God will be hid in the servant, and the Son of man will be manifest, because to Him “hath He given authority to execute judgment, because He is the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because He alone will appear in the form of a servant, but the Father not, since He has not taken upon Him the form of a servant; for that reason He saith above: “The Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly then had it been deferred, that the propounder might Himself be the interpreter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For before it was hidden; now, as I think, it is already manifest, that “He gave Him authority to execute judgment,” that “the Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son:” because the judgment is to be by that form which the Father hath not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what kind of judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Marvel not at this, for the hour is coming:” not that which now is, for the souls to rise; but that which is to be, for the bodies to rise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Him declare this more distinctly, that the heretical denier of the resurrection of the body may not find a pretext for sophistical cavil, although the meaning already shines out clearly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When it was said above, “The hour is coming,” He added, “and now is;” but just now, “The hour is coming,” He has not added, “and now is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Him, however, by the open truth, burst asunder all handles, all loops and pegs of sophistical attack, all the nooses of ensnaring objections.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in which all that are in the graves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more evident? what more distinct?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bodies are in the graves; souls are not in the graves, either of just or of unjust.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul of the just man was in the bosom of Abraham; the unjust man’s soul was in hell, tormented: neither the one nor the other was in the grave.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Above, when He saith, “The hour is coming, and now is,” I beseech you give earnest heed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye know, brethren, that we get the bread of the belly with toil; with how much greater toil the bread of the mind!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With labor you stand and hear, but with greater we stand and speak.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we labor for your sake, you ought to labor with us for your own sake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Above, then, when He said, “The hour is coming,” and added, “and now is,” what did He subjoin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God, and they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not say, “All the dead shall hear, and they that hear shall live;” for He meant the unrighteous to be understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And is it so, that all the unrighteous obey the gospel?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle says openly, “But not all obey the gospel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they that hear shall live, because all that obey the gospel shall pass to eternal life by faith: yet all do not obey; and this is now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But certainly, in the end, “All that are in the graves,” both the just and the unjust, “shall hear His voice, and come forth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is it He would not say, “and shall live”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All, indeed, will come forth, but all will not live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in that which He said above, “And they that hear shall live,” He meant it to be understood that there is in that very hearing and obeying an eternal and blessed life, which not all that shall come forth from the graves will have.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, then, both in the mention of graves, and by the expression of a “coming forth” from the graves, we openly understand a resurrection of bodies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “All shall hear His voice, and shall come forth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And where is judgment, if all shall hear and all shall come forth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is as if all were confusion; I see no distinguishing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly Thou hast received authority to judge, because Thou art the Son of man: behold, Thou wilt be present in the judgment; the bodies will rise again; but tell us something of the judgment itself, that is, of the separation of the evil and the good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear this further, then: “They that have done good into the resurrection of life; they that have done evil into the resurrection of judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When above He spoke of a resurrection of minds and souls, did He make any distinction?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No, for all “that hear shall live;” because by hearing, viz. by obeying, shall they live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But certainly not all will go to eternal life by rising and coming forth from the graves,—only they that have done well; and they that have done ill, to judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For here He has put judgment for punishment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There will also be a separation, not such as there is now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For now we are separated, not by place, but by character, affections, desires, faith, hope, charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now we live together with the unjust, though the life of all is not the same: in secret we are distinguished, in secret we are separated; as grain on the floor, not as grain in the granary.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On the floor, grain is both separated and mixed: separated, because severed from the chaff; mixed, because not yet winnowed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then there will be an open separation; a distinguishing of life just as of the character, a separation as there is in wisdom, so also will there be in bodies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They that have done well will go to live with the angels of God; they that have done evil, to be tormented with the devil and his angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the form of a servant will pass away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to this end He had manifested Himself, that He might execute judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After the judgment, He shall go hence, will lead with Him the body of which He is the head, and deliver up the kingdom of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then will openly be seen that form of God which could not be seen by the wicked, to whose vision the form of a servant must be shown.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says also in another place on this wise: “These shall go away into everlasting burning” (speaking of certain on the left), “but the just into life eternal;” of which life He says in another place: “And this is eternal life, that they may know Thee the one true God, and Jesus Christ whom Thou hast sent.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then will He be there manifested, “who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then He will manifest Himself, as He has promised to manifest Himself to them that love Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “he that loveth me,” saith He, “keepeth my commandments; and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father; and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was present in person with those to whom He was speaking: but they saw the form of a servant, they did not see the form of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were being led on His own beast to His dwelling to be healed; but now being healed, they will see, because, saith He, “I will manifest myself to him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is He shown equal to the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He says to Philip, “He that seeth me seeth my Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I cannot of myself do anything: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Else we might have said to Him, “Thou wilt judge, and the Father will not judge, for ‘all judgment hath He given to the Son;’ it is not, therefore, according to the Father that Thou wilt judge.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence He added, “I cannot of myself do anything: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not my own will, but the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Undoubtedly the Son quickeneth whom He will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He seeketh not His own will, but the will of Him that sent Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not my own, my proper will; not mine, not the Son of man’s; not mine to resist God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For men do their own will, not God’s, when they do what they list, not what God commands; but when they do what they list, so as yet to follow God’s will, they do not their own will, notwithstanding they do what they list to do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do what thou art bidden willingly, and thus shall thou both do what thou willest, and also not do thine own will, but His that biddeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As I hear, I judge.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son “heareth,” and the Father “showeth” to Him, and the Son seeth the Father doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we had deferred these matters, in order to handle them, so far as might lie in our abilities, with somewhat greater plainness and fullness, should time and strength remain to us after finishing the perusal of the passage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I say that I am able to speak yet further, you perhaps are not able to go on hearing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, perhaps, in your eagerness to hear, you say, “We are able.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Better, then, that I should confess my weakness, that, being already fatigued, I am not able to speak longer, than that, when you are already satiated, I should continue to pour into you what you cannot well digest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, as to this promise, which I deferred until today, should there be an opportunity, hold me, with the Lord’s help, your debtor until to-morrow.
